tipRules.tip: Johnston: On the St. Lawrence, fish an 80-yard stretch of bottom in about 40 feet targeting a pod of giant smallmouth. Catch one, then leave the spot and make a big run away before returning, because pressured deepwater smallmouth shut down after each catch.

knowledge.insight: Johnston: Pressured St. Lawrence smallmouth are extremely sensitive. Catch one and they shut down. Time and distance are the best remedy - make a big run away from the spot between catches. It's like night and day from even two years ago.
Johnston: Use Garmin LiveScope to track down deep smallmouth and make the perfect cast. In rough conditions with big waves, efficiency drops because it's harder to use LiveScope accurately.

Johnston: Use a four-bait rotation for picky pressured smallmouth. Figured out something about rigging and presentation that makes the difference - it has to do with how you rig and present the bait, not just what bait you use.
